data.text: Nvidia shares fall as investors fret over returns, look past strong results
data.snippet: Nvidia's shares fell on Thursday as investors looked past strong earnings, wary that the chip designer continues to channel capital into expanding the AI ecosystem, the payoffs for which are still unclear, rather than boosting shareholder returns.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Illustration shows Nvidia logo

relatedHeadlines.text: Nvidia's CEO prepares investors for a renewed battle with Intel, AMD
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Nvidia <NVDA.O> may have made its immense fortune on the back of specialized graphics processing units (GPUs) used to power artificial intelligence servers, but CEO Jensen Huang is increasingly professing his love for the more generalist CPU.

relatedHeadlines.text: Nvidia is dogged by memories of cycles past
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Nvidia’s <NVDA.O> chips are essential for artificial intelligence. It's unsurprising, then, that on Wednesday it reported selling a lot of them. In the quarter ending January 25, revenue expanded  https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-202673% https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-2026 year-over-year to $68 billion, as demand grows at a seemingly insatiable rate. The problem for the $4.8 trillion technology titan is that investors seem skeptical that the good times will last.

data.text: Berkshire CEO Abel to lay out thinking for a post-Buffett world
data.snippet: Berkshire Hathaway's new Chief Executive Greg Abel faces numerous challenges as the successor to famed billionaire Warren Buffett.

image.text: Berkshire Hathaway Inc annual shareholders' meeting in Omaha, Nebraska

data.text: Warner Bros’ TV decline puts pressure on Netflix deal
data.snippet: Warner Bros Discovery heaped an additional burden on Netflix's $82.7 billion bid for the movie studio and HBO.

image.text: The Warner Bros. studio lot in California

data.text: Exclusive-Broadcom expects to sell 1 million 3D stacked chips by 2027
data.snippet: Artificial intelligence chip designer Broadcom said that it expects to sell at least 1 million chips by 2027 based on its stacked design tech, an executive told Reuters on Wednesday.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Illustration shows Broadcom logo and computer motherboard

data.text: J.M. Smucker adds two directors after private discussions with activist Elliott, shares jump
data.snippet: J.M. Smucker reached an agreement with activist hedge fund Elliott Investment Management to add two executives with financial and food industry experience to its board to help the jelly, peanut butter and coffee maker improve operations.

image.text: Smucker's marmalade and preserve, a brand owned by The J.M. Smucker Company, is seen for sale in a store in Manhattan, New York City

data.text: Exclusive-Rare earth shortages worsen in US aerospace, chips despite trade truce, sources say
data.snippet: Suppliers to U.S. aerospace and semiconductor firms face worsening rare earth shortages, with two turning away some clients, industry insiders said, weeks before U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping for a summit in Beijing.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Rare earth shortages worsen in US aerospace, chips despite trade truce, sources say

data.text: Walmart agrees to pay $100 million to settle deceptive earnings charges, FTC says
data.snippet: Walmart has agreed to pay $100 million to settle charges that the company caused delivery drivers to lose tens of millions of dollars in earnings, the U.S. Federal Trade Commission said in a statement on Thursday.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Customers leave a Walmart Supercenter retail store in North Bergen, New Jersey

data.text: Exclusive-CNBC to unify digital and TV news operations, cut nearly a dozen jobs, sources say
data.snippet: CNBC is restructuring its newsroom to unify its TV and digital operations in a move that would result in nearly a dozen layoffs including the departure of the website's managing editor, Jeff McCracken, four sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

data.text: Wall St slides as tech rally stalls, AI enthusiasm wanes after Nvidia results
data.snippet: U.S. stock indexes turned sharply lower on Thursday, the day after a less-than-stellar earnings report from artificial intelligence vanguard Nvidia dragged technology shares which have provided muscle to the recent rally.

image.text: Traders work on the floor at the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in New York City

data.text: Musk touts California robotaxis but Tesla does nothing to get permits
data.snippet: For more than a year, Elon Musk has repeatedly said Tesla is months away from launching a driverless robotaxi service in California – once state regulators give their blessing.

image.text: Tesla robotaxis launch in Austin, Texas

data.text: Apple seeks dismissal of fraud lawsuit over Siri AI, Epic injunction
data.snippet: Apple urged a federal judge to dismiss a proposed class action claiming it defrauded shareholders twice, by overstating the artificial intelligence capabilities of its Siri voice assistant and falsely representing its compliance with an injunction governing commissions on app sales.

image.text: People walk past an Apple logo at an Apple store in Paris

data.text: Google rolls out Nano Banana 2 after viral success of AI image generation tool
data.snippet: Google on Thursday unveiled a successor to its viral Nano Banana image generation model, touting faster performance as the search-engine behemoth pushes to attract more users to its AI tools.

image.text: Pedestrian walks past the Google offices in London

data.text: Judge rejects Kenvue bid to dismiss Texas lawsuit over Tylenol safety
data.snippet: A Texas judge on Thursday rejected Kenvue's bid to dismiss a lawsuit by state Attorney General Ken Paxton accusing the company of failing to warn consumers that it may be unsafe for pregnant women to take Tylenol.

image.text: Kenvue Inc, Johnson & Johnson's consumer-health business, IPO at the NYSE in New York

data.text: Novavax raises revenue forecast on boost from vaccine deals, shares soar
data.snippet: Novavax raised its adjusted revenue forecast for 2026 on Thursday, banking on milestone payments from licensing and supply deals for its shots, although it noted that the current regulatory environment posed a risk for vaccine companies.

image.text: An illustration photo shows a dose of Novavax Nuvaxovid COVID-19 vaccine in Schwenksville

data.text: BREAKINGVIEWS-Ellisons tempt Netflix into a game of M&A chicken
data.snippet: Price is one thing; certainty is another. That’s the message from David Ellison, boss of ‘Mission: Impossible’ studio Paramount Skydance. On Tuesday, the company confirmed a new, higher offer for Hollywood conglomerate Warner Bros Discovery, raising its $30-a-share proposal to $31. More importantly, Ellison offered greater security around a toppy debt load. It’s a risky move, but puts the onus on rival Netflix to assume some danger of its own.

image.text: Paramount Global merger with Skydance Media almost complete
